item.title: 做到這些，再說超越了iPhone！安卓VS蘋果拍照究竟誰更好？｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:title: 做到這些，再說超越了iPhone！安卓VS蘋果拍照究竟誰更好？｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:author: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL
item.itunes:summary:
值得閱讀的理由

  • 深入了解當前Android旗艦手機相機與iPhone在拍照及錄影上的優劣對比。
  • 揭示部分Android品牌在AI處理、色彩演算、預覽一致性等方面存在的具體問題。
  • 認識iPhone相機在色彩穩定性、錄影防手震、儲存效率等常被忽略的優勢。

摘要

引言：iPhone相機不再是最佳？作者的論點

作者開場即挑戰了當今「iPhone相機不再是最佳」的普遍看法，並表達了對當前Android旗艦手機拍照表現的不滿。他聲稱要列舉這些Android旗艦相機的「十大罪狀」，涵蓋了拍照、錄影、原生與第三方應用等多個方面，累積了許多不吐不快的使用經驗。

長焦與AI處理：過度演算與隨機生成

作者首先從長焦鏡頭的比較說起。他原本預期規格強大的Android旗艦會輕鬆取勝，結果卻出乎意料。雖然近年來AI的加入讓超長焦照片變得較為「可用」，尤其是在拍攝規律物體時，但作者認為這種過度處理讓照片看起來「太過了」，背景本應模糊卻被AI強制銳化。

接著，作者特別點名Honor，批評其AI的「隨機生成」現象，例如將鳥變成直升機。他將其與Lightroom、Hasselblad、Capture ONE等專業軟體的AI功能（如降噪、皮膚平滑）進行對比，強調AI的應用應是輔助和優化，而非憑空捏造。作者呼籲手機製造商不應為了賣點而犧牲AI的可用性。

色彩演算問題：塑膠感與不穩定性

作者指出手機拍照常追求討喜的色彩，提升陰影和飽和度，但若控制不好容易產生「塑膠感」。他具體批評了：OPPO在亮處顯得平淡，增加對比又太暗；vivo的紋理與光線組合常顯得「髒」，尤其拍貓時，且預覽與最終成像差異大；Xiaomi的色彩會隨OTA更新改變，經典模式容易死黑但中間調缺乏層次，鮮豔模式又過於塑膠化，缺乏居中的選項。

所見即所得？預覽與成品的落差

關於「所見即所得」的討論，作者發現OPPO和Xiaomi在日常使用中預覽與成像相當接近，但Xiaomi更傾向允許過曝或欠曝，OPPO則較能保留整體觀感。然而，vivo為了讓最終成品討喜，有時後製處理很重，導致用戶手動調整曝光時，畫面反而變亮。iPhone在此方面幾乎沒有變化，作者認為這可能意味著其預覽即已是重度處理後的結果，或者缺乏細緻的即時調整能力。

iPhone的穩定性與生態：色彩一致性的重要

作者認為iPhone的升級雖然緩慢但穩定，尤其在色彩還原上代際間保持高度一致。他強調這點的重要性，特別是在第三方濾鏡應用方面。許多在小紅書上流行的濾鏡，在Android手機上不僅顏色會跑掉，畫質也嚴重下降，尤其Xiaomi的噪點問題嚴重。這種不一致性使得用戶購買濾鏡後換手機體驗會打折。因此，Android廠商轉為將濾鏡內建於原生相機應用中，顯示了其生態依賴自身的傾向。

錄影表現與儲存效率：被忽略的優勢

關於錄影，作者簡要提及iPhone的傳統優勢（如防手震），並展示即使在夜間低光源環境下，iPhone的動態範圍也能與進光量更大的Android旗艦匹敵。他也點出vivo X200 Ultra等新機在夜間4K錄影時存在噪點多或畫面模糊的問題。

接著，作者談到實際成本——儲存空間。iPhone照片的檔案大小明顯小很多，幾乎只有Android的一半，原因在於iPhone支援高效率儲存格式（HEIF）。這一實用功能在Android陣營中十分罕見，且啟用後常有限制（如禁用Live Photo、失去水印功能），作者認為其普及度太低。

總結：賣點至上還是用戶體驗至上？

作者預期會被指責是「果粉」，但他解釋並非一味讚揚Apple，而是指出Android廠商可以學習的特質。身為重度攝影用戶，他使用Android旗艦幾乎都需訂閱雲端空間。他認為製造商可能最不看重「效果本身」，而是追逐「亮眼賣點」。這種不追求穩定效果、OTA更新就能改變色彩表現的做法，導致用戶體驗缺乏一致性，讓人難以安心。作者最後強調，在競爭激烈的市場中，追求「用戶友善體驗」而非僅是「出眾效果」，應該成為主流目標。

================================================

The bananas shot by iPhone all look kinda greenish,. like they're made of cement or something. Our color reproduction is super accurate,. and the image quality is really clean. Oh my god,. ever since I started using smartphones,. Huawei and Xiaomi have been constantly compared to Apple. Their night mode is way ahead of the competition! Seriously,. how many years has it been now that Chinese phones have been outperforming Apple? But now,. it seems like the tables have turned. Who still dares to say iPhone has the best camera? I do! I'm not kidding. Don't get me wrong,. Android flagships are powerful,. but iPhone isn't a pushover either. I've been waiting for so many generations of the Ultra models,. and today, I just can't hold it in anymore. Let me list out the ten sins of these Android camera flagships. From photography to videography,. from first-party apps to third-party ones,. I've had enough! It all started. when I casually compared the telephoto lenses. of these phones with my iPhone. I thought, with all those. crazy specs these Chinese flagships are packing,. it's a no-brainer that they'd crush the competition. But the result? It shattered my expectations. To be fair,. the recent addition of AI. has indeed made ultra-telephoto shots. somewhat usable,. from being completely unwatchable. Especially with. regular patterns like walls. or standard scenes.

like trees and grass. At first glance,. they seem okay,. but my life isn't that regular, you know? Look at this one. The iPhone's image quality is indeed poor,. but when I put up the shots from other phones,. the overprocessed look. is just too much. The background is supposed to be blurred,. but AI forcefully sharpens it back. Oh yeah,. speaking of AI,. I gotta bring in a wildcard here. Honor takes the cake for being the most outrageous. What's this? How does this random thing just pop up out of nowhere? Like, a "failed man" or turning a little bird into a helicopter? It's all just wild imagination. And even if I give them the benefit of the doubt,. it's not like their shots are any clearer than the others, right? At this point, someone might jump up and say,. "Hey, all phones use AI now. Does that mean AI-generated photos don't count as photography?" Well,. not necessarily. Lightroom is pretty professional, isn't it? Yet they added AI noise reduction a few years back,. and it was well-received. Hasselblad's no slouch either; their phone app also includes AI noise reduction. Even Phase One, with cameras costing hundreds of thousands,. updated their software Capture ONE. with AI skin smoothing. But adaptation isn't about making things up randomly. Phone manufacturers,. don't just go all out for those selling points. and end up compromising the usability of AI. At least let me be willing to keep it turned on, okay?

We can draw similar conclusions about color algorithms. Phone photography. always aims to be pleasing,. boosting shadows and saturation. But if not controlled well,. it can easily give off that plasticky look. Here, I'll just point out some common pitfalls. With OPPO,. if a scene is bright,. it tends to look flat. But add a bit of contrast,. and it becomes overly dark. With vivo,. the combination of textures and lighting. often just makes things look dirty,. especially when shooting cats. It's super dirty. And this often happens. even in broad daylight. The preview and the final shot are nothing alike. As for Xiaomi,. it's a bit tricky. A simple OTA update,. and the colors can change. I'm just talking about its performance. before this video was made. In its classic mode,. it tends to crush the blacks really hard,. but the mid-tones. don't actually have that much depth. Sometimes, it gives off this. weird vibe where it feels both textured and plasticky at the same time,. like a toy car. But if you switch to the vivid mode,. the colors become overly plasticky. Neither mode is very versatile. A lot of times, I wish there was. a third mode in between. Oh yeah, speaking of. "what you see is what you get,"

which was a common saying a few years back,. let's see how things stand now. First off, it's quite surprising that. OPPO and Xiaomi can almost. match the preview in daily use. But I feel like. Xiaomi is more willing to let the image be overexposed. or underexposed. OPPO, on the other hand,. tends to preserve the overall look more. But vivo,. to make the final shot more appealing,. goes pretty hard. on post-processing in some cases. Actually, that's kind of understandable. Actually, that's kind of understandable. Vivo does often come out looking the most pleasing. But sometimes,. I just want to enhance the atmosphere a bit,. make it darker. I pull it down,. and oh, it gets brighter. I pull it down some more,. and it brightens up again. I pull it even further. With iPhone. this is the result. Don't wait! It's already processed. There's just no change. Besides these most obvious differences,. those who've been keeping an eye on iPhone know that. while its upgrades may be slow,. they're steady. Their ability has been gradually increasing generation by generation,. and the pixel count has been steadily rising. More importantly,. the color reproduction remains remarkably consistent across each iteration. Well. This has a far greater impact than you might think.

Just look at those popular filter apps. You see one on Red Note,. and you're like,. "Wow, there's an Android version!" But when you try it,. is it really the same filter? Not only are the colors different,. but the image quality degrades severely. Especially with Xiaomi,. the noise is so bad it's like stars in the sky. That's a real problem. If I love a particular filter,. pay for it,. and then switch phones only to find the colors change instantly,. that's a bit of a letdown, isn't it? So now, Android manufacturers. are simply integrating various filter effects. into their native camera apps. Look at OPPO's Pro mode; with a bit of tweaking, you can get shots like this. Vivo has a bunch of filters and AI effects,. and Xiaomi's Pro mode offers. unique stylization options. This solution. is indeed a viable approach. After all, in the Android world,. it's often better to rely on yourself than on others. Oh yeah,. I wonder if anyone knows this. On Pinduoduo,. there are actually a ton of sellers offering Xiaomi filter presets. for just a few cents each. What kind of business model is that? As for iPhone's traditional strength in videography, I won't delve into it in detail this time. I've even made a whole video about it before. Even for basic image stabilization,. iPhone is miles ahead. With Android phones, you might think you've captured a shot,. but in reality, you haven't. But what if I told you that even for nighttime videography,.

the iPhone, with its relatively small form factor,. isn't intimidated by those "monsters" with 50% more light intake? Let's take a look. Even at night,. the iPhone's dynamic range. holds its own against these new flagships. When it gets really dark,. like in this spot. where the only light source is that distant streetlamp,. the iPhone is indeed dark,. but are those Ultra models. really doing much better? And the X200 Ultra. has exposed a new issue:. while 4K30 is bright,. the noise is terrible,. and 4K60 is blurry. If the previous points were about user experience,. then storage space. is where real money comes into play! Gold storage isn't something you can splurge on,. but look at the sizes. iPhone's storage is always significantly smaller. Almost every photo. is roughly half the size. The reason is simple:. that is. iPhone supports high-efficiency storage. Take the same photo; with high-efficiency storage enabled,. you can save almost half the space instantly. So, who among Android phones. has such a handy feature? Only two of them,. and even then, once you enable Live Photo,. it's no longer usable. Xiaomi even loses its watermark removal function. It's just that the popularity of this feature is still too low. Actually, after all this discussion,. some of you.

might be thinking,. "This guy's just an Apple fanboy,. case closed!" emm. Well, I might be praising Apple,. but that's not my intention. Even if we single out iPhone's photography,. it's not my favorite. However, iPhone still possesses some qualities. that all Android manufacturers can learn from. As a heavy photography user,. I have to subscribe to cloud storage. for almost every flagship phone that emphasizes photography; otherwise, I'd run out of space in no time. But as I use these phones, I realize that. for manufacturers, the effect might not be the most crucial factor. Instead, everyone is chasing after those eye-catching selling points. I'm not targeting anyone here,. so don't take it personally! In fact,. it seems that manufacturers don't even have their own firm stance on what constitutes a good effect. It's perfectly normal for an OTA update. to completely change. your phone's color reproduction. How can such an inconsistent experience. make users feel at ease? Even if they're criticized for negative optimization,. can you really blame anyone? Now that the competition has reached such a ridiculous level,. even for mid-range phones' photography capabilities,. the pursuit of a user-friendly experience. rather than just outstanding effects. should. no longer be a niche demand. OK~. So that's all for this video. If you find this video interesting. Remember to hit that one-click Like, Subscribe & Bell Notification for me! This is very important to me.

Then I'm Cherry. See you next time. Bye ~~.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NlZPs8zP7o8

歡迎訂閱切狸~ 在這裡你會看到很多有趣的數碼資訊哦！ | Please don’t forget to subscribe 💋：https://bit.ly/3jQ3429

-------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------

別看現在安卓旗艦們都「身強力壯」的，我等了這麼多代Ultra了，今天真是忍不了了，列一列安卓拍照旗艦的十宗罪！

往期視頻：

https://youtu.be/SoFQHTQEG-w

https://youtu.be/SWYCfjATo3s

https://youtu.be/O5pabQt5njE

Xiaomi is more willing to let the image be overexposed. or underexposed. OPPO, on the other hand,. tends to preserve the overall look more. But vivo,. to make the final shot more appealing,. goes pretty hard. on post-processing in some cases. Actually, that's kind of understandable. Actually, that's kind of understandable. Vivo does often come out looking the most pleasing. But sometimes,. I just want to enhance the atmosphere a bit,. make it darker. I pull it down,. and oh, it gets brighter. I pull it down some more,. and it brightens up again. I pull it even further. With iPhone. this is the result. Don't wait! It's already processed. There's just no change. Besides these most obvious differences,. those who've been keeping an eye on iPhone know that. while its upgrades may be slow,. they're steady. Their ability has been gradually increasing generation by generation,. and the pixel count has been steadily rising. More importantly,. the color reproduction remains remarkably consistent across each iteration. Well. This has a far greater impact than you might think.

Just look at those popular filter apps. You see one on Red Note,. and you're like,. "Wow, there's an Android version!" But when you try it,. is it really the same filter? Not only are the colors different,. but the image quality degrades severely. Especially with Xiaomi,. the noise is so bad it's like stars in the sky. That's a real problem. If I love a particular filter,. pay for it,. and then switch phones only to find the colors change instantly,. that's a bit of a letdown, isn't it? So now, Android manufacturers. are simply integrating various filter effects. into their native camera apps. Look at OPPO's Pro mode; with a bit of tweaking, you can get shots like this. Vivo has a bunch of filters and AI effects,. and Xiaomi's Pro mode offers. unique stylization options. This solution. is indeed a viable approach. After all, in the Android world,. it's often better to rely on yourself than on others. Oh yeah,. I wonder if anyone knows this. On Pinduoduo,. there are actually a ton of sellers offering Xiaomi filter presets. for just a few cents each. What kind of business model is that? As for iPhone's traditional strength in videography, I won't delve into it in detail this time. I've even made a whole video about it before. Even for basic image stabilization,. iPhone is miles ahead. With Android phones, you might think you've captured a shot,. but in reality, you haven't. But what if I told you that even for nighttime videography,.

the iPhone, with its relatively small form factor,. isn't intimidated by those "monsters" with 50% more light intake? Let's take a look. Even at night,. the iPhone's dynamic range. holds its own against these new flagships. When it gets really dark,. like in this spot. where the only light source is that distant streetlamp,. the iPhone is indeed dark,. but are those Ultra models. really doing much better? And the X200 Ultra. has exposed a new issue:. while 4K30 is bright,. the noise is terrible,. and 4K60 is blurry. If the previous points were about user experience,. then storage space. is where real money comes into play! Gold storage isn't something you can splurge on,. but look at the sizes. iPhone's storage is always significantly smaller. Almost every photo. is roughly half the size. The reason is simple:. that is. iPhone supports high-efficiency storage. Take the same photo; with high-efficiency storage enabled,. you can save almost half the space instantly. So, who among Android phones. has such a handy feature? Only two of them,. and even then, once you enable Live Photo,. it's no longer usable. Xiaomi even loses its watermark removal function. It's just that the popularity of this feature is still too low. Actually, after all this discussion,. some of you.

might be thinking,. "This guy's just an Apple fanboy,. case closed!" emm. Well, I might be praising Apple,. but that's not my intention. Even if we single out iPhone's photography,. it's not my favorite. However, iPhone still possesses some qualities. that all Android manufacturers can learn from. As a heavy photography user,. I have to subscribe to cloud storage. for almost every flagship phone that emphasizes photography; otherwise, I'd run out of space in no time. But as I use these phones, I realize that. for manufacturers, the effect might not be the most crucial factor. Instead, everyone is chasing after those eye-catching selling points. I'm not targeting anyone here,. so don't take it personally! In fact,. it seems that manufacturers don't even have their own firm stance on what constitutes a good effect. It's perfectly normal for an OTA update. to completely change. your phone's color reproduction. How can such an inconsistent experience. make users feel at ease? Even if they're criticized for negative optimization,. can you really blame anyone? Now that the competition has reached such a ridiculous level,. even for mid-range phones' photography capabilities,. the pursuit of a user-friendly experience. rather than just outstanding effects. should. no longer be a niche demand. OK~. So that's all for this video. If you find this video interesting. Remember to hit that one-click Like, Subscribe & Bell Notification for me! This is very important to me.

Then I'm Cherry. See you next time. Bye ~~.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NlZPs8zP7o8

item.content:encoded:
值得閱讀的理由

  • 深入了解當前Android旗艦手機相機與iPhone在拍照及錄影上的優劣對比。
  • 揭示部分Android品牌在AI處理、色彩演算、預覽一致性等方面存在的具體問題。
  • 認識iPhone相機在色彩穩定性、錄影防手震、儲存效率等常被忽略的優勢。

摘要

引言：iPhone相機不再是最佳？作者的論點

作者開場即挑戰了當今「iPhone相機不再是最佳」的普遍看法，並表達了對當前Android旗艦手機拍照表現的不滿。他聲稱要列舉這些Android旗艦相機的「十大罪狀」，涵蓋了拍照、錄影、原生與第三方應用等多個方面，累積了許多不吐不快的使用經驗。

長焦與AI處理：過度演算與隨機生成

作者首先從長焦鏡頭的比較說起。他原本預期規格強大的Android旗艦會輕鬆取勝，結果卻出乎意料。雖然近年來AI的加入讓超長焦照片變得較為「可用」，尤其是在拍攝規律物體時，但作者認為這種過度處理讓照片看起來「太過了」，背景本應模糊卻被AI強制銳化。

接著，作者特別點名Honor，批評其AI的「隨機生成」現象，例如將鳥變成直升機。他將其與Lightroom、Hasselblad、Capture ONE等專業軟體的AI功能（如降噪、皮膚平滑）進行對比，強調AI的應用應是輔助和優化，而非憑空捏造。作者呼籲手機製造商不應為了賣點而犧牲AI的可用性。

色彩演算問題：塑膠感與不穩定性

作者指出手機拍照常追求討喜的色彩，提升陰影和飽和度，但若控制不好容易產生「塑膠感」。他具體批評了：OPPO在亮處顯得平淡，增加對比又太暗；vivo的紋理與光線組合常顯得「髒」，尤其拍貓時，且預覽與最終成像差異大；Xiaomi的色彩會隨OTA更新改變，經典模式容易死黑但中間調缺乏層次，鮮豔模式又過於塑膠化，缺乏居中的選項。

所見即所得？預覽與成品的落差

關於「所見即所得」的討論，作者發現OPPO和Xiaomi在日常使用中預覽與成像相當接近，但Xiaomi更傾向允許過曝或欠曝，OPPO則較能保留整體觀感。然而，vivo為了讓最終成品討喜，有時後製處理很重，導致用戶手動調整曝光時，畫面反而變亮。iPhone在此方面幾乎沒有變化，作者認為這可能意味著其預覽即已是重度處理後的結果，或者缺乏細緻的即時調整能力。

iPhone的穩定性與生態：色彩一致性的重要

作者認為iPhone的升級雖然緩慢但穩定，尤其在色彩還原上代際間保持高度一致。他強調這點的重要性，特別是在第三方濾鏡應用方面。許多在小紅書上流行的濾鏡，在Android手機上不僅顏色會跑掉，畫質也嚴重下降，尤其Xiaomi的噪點問題嚴重。這種不一致性使得用戶購買濾鏡後換手機體驗會打折。因此，Android廠商轉為將濾鏡內建於原生相機應用中，顯示了其生態依賴自身的傾向。

錄影表現與儲存效率：被忽略的優勢

關於錄影，作者簡要提及iPhone的傳統優勢（如防手震），並展示即使在夜間低光源環境下，iPhone的動態範圍也能與進光量更大的Android旗艦匹敵。他也點出vivo X200 Ultra等新機在夜間4K錄影時存在噪點多或畫面模糊的問題。

接著，作者談到實際成本——儲存空間。iPhone照片的檔案大小明顯小很多，幾乎只有Android的一半，原因在於iPhone支援高效率儲存格式（HEIF）。這一實用功能在Android陣營中十分罕見，且啟用後常有限制（如禁用Live Photo、失去水印功能），作者認為其普及度太低。

總結：賣點至上還是用戶體驗至上？

作者預期會被指責是「果粉」，但他解釋並非一味讚揚Apple，而是指出Android廠商可以學習的特質。身為重度攝影用戶，他使用Android旗艦幾乎都需訂閱雲端空間。他認為製造商可能最不看重「效果本身」，而是追逐「亮眼賣點」。這種不追求穩定效果、OTA更新就能改變色彩表現的做法，導致用戶體驗缺乏一致性，讓人難以安心。作者最後強調，在競爭激烈的市場中，追求「用戶友善體驗」而非僅是「出眾效果」，應該成為主流目標。

================================================

The bananas shot by iPhone all look kinda greenish,. like they're made of cement or something. Our color reproduction is super accurate,. and the image quality is really clean. Oh my god,. ever since I started using smartphones,. Huawei and Xiaomi have been constantly compared to Apple. Their night mode is way ahead of the competition! Seriously,. how many years has it been now that Chinese phones have been outperforming Apple? But now,. it seems like the tables have turned. Who still dares to say iPhone has the best camera? I do! I'm not kidding. Don't get me wrong,. Android flagships are powerful,. but iPhone isn't a pushover either. I've been waiting for so many generations of the Ultra models,. and today, I just can't hold it in anymore. Let me list out the ten sins of these Android camera flagships. From photography to videography,. from first-party apps to third-party ones,. I've had enough! It all started. when I casually compared the telephoto lenses. of these phones with my iPhone. I thought, with all those. crazy specs these Chinese flagships are packing,. it's a no-brainer that they'd crush the competition. But the result? It shattered my expectations. To be fair,. the recent addition of AI. has indeed made ultra-telephoto shots. somewhat usable,. from being completely unwatchable. Especially with. regular patterns like walls. or standard scenes.

like trees and grass. At first glance,. they seem okay,. but my life isn't that regular, you know? Look at this one. The iPhone's image quality is indeed poor,. but when I put up the shots from other phones,. the overprocessed look. is just too much. The background is supposed to be blurred,. but AI forcefully sharpens it back. Oh yeah,. speaking of AI,. I gotta bring in a wildcard here. Honor takes the cake for being the most outrageous. What's this? How does this random thing just pop up out of nowhere? Like, a "failed man" or turning a little bird into a helicopter? It's all just wild imagination. And even if I give them the benefit of the doubt,. it's not like their shots are any clearer than the others, right? At this point, someone might jump up and say,. "Hey, all phones use AI now. Does that mean AI-generated photos don't count as photography?" Well,. not necessarily. Lightroom is pretty professional, isn't it? Yet they added AI noise reduction a few years back,. and it was well-received. Hasselblad's no slouch either; their phone app also includes AI noise reduction. Even Phase One, with cameras costing hundreds of thousands,. updated their software Capture ONE. with AI skin smoothing. But adaptation isn't about making things up randomly. Phone manufacturers,. don't just go all out for those selling points. and end up compromising the usability of AI. At least let me be willing to keep it turned on, okay?

We can draw similar conclusions about color algorithms. Phone photography. always aims to be pleasing,. boosting shadows and saturation. But if not controlled well,. it can easily give off that plasticky look. Here, I'll just point out some common pitfalls. With OPPO,. if a scene is bright,. it tends to look flat. But add a bit of contrast,. and it becomes overly dark. With vivo,. the combination of textures and lighting. often just makes things look dirty,. especially when shooting cats. It's super dirty. And this often happens. even in broad daylight. The preview and the final shot are nothing alike. As for Xiaomi,. it's a bit tricky. A simple OTA update,. and the colors can change. I'm just talking about its performance. before this video was made. In its classic mode,. it tends to crush the blacks really hard,. but the mid-tones. don't actually have that much depth. Sometimes, it gives off this. weird vibe where it feels both textured and plasticky at the same time,. like a toy car. But if you switch to the vivid mode,. the colors become overly plasticky. Neither mode is very versatile. A lot of times, I wish there was. a third mode in between. Oh yeah, speaking of. "what you see is what you get,"

which was a common saying a few years back,. let's see how things stand now. First off, it's quite surprising that. OPPO and Xiaomi can almost. match the preview in daily use. But I feel like. Xiaomi is more willing to let the image be overexposed. or underexposed. OPPO, on the other hand,. tends to preserve the overall look more. But vivo,. to make the final shot more appealing,. goes pretty hard. on post-processing in some cases. Actually, that's kind of understandable. Actually, that's kind of understandable. Vivo does often come out looking the most pleasing. But sometimes,. I just want to enhance the atmosphere a bit,. make it darker. I pull it down,. and oh, it gets brighter. I pull it down some more,. and it brightens up again. I pull it even further. With iPhone. this is the result. Don't wait! It's already processed. There's just no change. Besides these most obvious differences,. those who've been keeping an eye on iPhone know that. while its upgrades may be slow,. they're steady. Their ability has been gradually increasing generation by generation,. and the pixel count has been steadily rising. More importantly,. the color reproduction remains remarkably consistent across each iteration. Well. This has a far greater impact than you might think.

Just look at those popular filter apps. You see one on Red Note,. and you're like,. "Wow, there's an Android version!" But when you try it,. is it really the same filter? Not only are the colors different,. but the image quality degrades severely. Especially with Xiaomi,. the noise is so bad it's like stars in the sky. That's a real problem. If I love a particular filter,. pay for it,. and then switch phones only to find the colors change instantly,. that's a bit of a letdown, isn't it? So now, Android manufacturers. are simply integrating various filter effects. into their native camera apps. Look at OPPO's Pro mode; with a bit of tweaking, you can get shots like this. Vivo has a bunch of filters and AI effects,. and Xiaomi's Pro mode offers. unique stylization options. This solution. is indeed a viable approach. After all, in the Android world,. it's often better to rely on yourself than on others. Oh yeah,. I wonder if anyone knows this. On Pinduoduo,. there are actually a ton of sellers offering Xiaomi filter presets. for just a few cents each. What kind of business model is that? As for iPhone's traditional strength in videography, I won't delve into it in detail this time. I've even made a whole video about it before. Even for basic image stabilization,. iPhone is miles ahead. With Android phones, you might think you've captured a shot,. but in reality, you haven't. But what if I told you that even for nighttime videography,.

the iPhone, with its relatively small form factor,. isn't intimidated by those "monsters" with 50% more light intake? Let's take a look. Even at night,. the iPhone's dynamic range. holds its own against these new flagships. When it gets really dark,. like in this spot. where the only light source is that distant streetlamp,. the iPhone is indeed dark,. but are those Ultra models. really doing much better? And the X200 Ultra. has exposed a new issue:. while 4K30 is bright,. the noise is terrible,. and 4K60 is blurry. If the previous points were about user experience,. then storage space. is where real money comes into play! Gold storage isn't something you can splurge on,. but look at the sizes. iPhone's storage is always significantly smaller. Almost every photo. is roughly half the size. The reason is simple:. that is. iPhone supports high-efficiency storage. Take the same photo; with high-efficiency storage enabled,. you can save almost half the space instantly. So, who among Android phones. has such a handy feature? Only two of them,. and even then, once you enable Live Photo,. it's no longer usable. Xiaomi even loses its watermark removal function. It's just that the popularity of this feature is still too low. Actually, after all this discussion,. some of you.

might be thinking,. "This guy's just an Apple fanboy,. case closed!" emm. Well, I might be praising Apple,. but that's not my intention. Even if we single out iPhone's photography,. it's not my favorite. However, iPhone still possesses some qualities. that all Android manufacturers can learn from. As a heavy photography user,. I have to subscribe to cloud storage. for almost every flagship phone that emphasizes photography; otherwise, I'd run out of space in no time. But as I use these phones, I realize that. for manufacturers, the effect might not be the most crucial factor. Instead, everyone is chasing after those eye-catching selling points. I'm not targeting anyone here,. so don't take it personally! In fact,. it seems that manufacturers don't even have their own firm stance on what constitutes a good effect. It's perfectly normal for an OTA update. to completely change. your phone's color reproduction. How can such an inconsistent experience. make users feel at ease? Even if they're criticized for negative optimization,. can you really blame anyone? Now that the competition has reached such a ridiculous level,. even for mid-range phones' photography capabilities,. the pursuit of a user-friendly experience. rather than just outstanding effects. should. no longer be a niche demand. OK~. So that's all for this video. If you find this video interesting. Remember to hit that one-click Like, Subscribe & Bell Notification for me! This is very important to me.

Then I'm Cherry. See you next time. Bye ~~.

=============

item.title: 把所有蘋果設備都更新了OS26之後，我好像走進了科技和人文的死胡同…｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:title: 把所有蘋果設備都更新了OS26之後，我好像走進了科技和人文的死胡同…｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:author: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL
item.itunes:summary:
值得閱讀的理由

  • 了解 Apple 全新的「液態玻璃」設計語言及其在不同裝置上的表現差異（尤其是在 iPhone 與 iPad 之間）。
  • 認識 iPadOS 在多視窗功能上的重大突破，以及其他系統（macOS, Spotlight, Photos App）的功能增強。
  • 探討 iPhone 在 Apple 生態系統中角色的可能轉變，從核心裝置轉向更像是終端與入口。
  • 獲得對本次 WWDC 更新的整體評價，包括其技術挑戰、潛在策略以及對未來發展方向的洞察（例如摺疊螢幕的可能性）。

摘要

這一次的 WWDC 引起了很高的討論度，作者提到這是近年來備受期待的一次。更新強行導入了全新的「液態玻璃」設計風格，作者及其工作室的同事認為雖然某些單獨的細節不錯，但整體組合起來顯得混亂，並且影響了可讀性。

設計在不同裝置上的呈現

作者覺得 iOS 26 系列的設計似乎是為大螢幕裝置而生，iPhone 在其中更像是戰略性犧牲品。作者觀察到，同樣的模糊效果和圖標呈現，在 iPad 上能更好地展現設計中的細緻光影、層次感和液態玻璃效果，而 iPhone 上的效果則顯得不夠充分且圖標過於擁擠。

設計靈感的猜測

關於設計靈感，官方宣稱來自 VisionOS。然而，身為 Vision Pro 使用者的作者對此提出質疑，認為實際材質呈現並不相似。作者推測這可能是為了帶動 Vision Pro 的流量，並大膽猜測其靈感實際上可能源自 HarmonyOS，並以類似的放大鏡和全局光效作為例證。

設備相容性與初步表現

令人意外的是，本次更新的設備相容性很高，從 iPhone 11 系列及以上都可以升級。作者用 iPhone 12 進行測試，發現這些折射效果、標籤頁中的小氣泡等細節呈現得相當不錯，儘管作為 Beta 1 版本偶爾會出現卡頓。

設計細節與圖標

作者認為 iOS 26 的設計解答了去年 iOS 18 隨機出現的圖標自定義功能之謎，一切都是為了搭配這次的玻璃效果。這些效果單獨看確實很漂亮，尤其文件夾看起來像是「應用琥珀」。蘋果的典型做法是，只要開發者遵循指南，系統就會自動轉換圖標風格，並且不會徵詢用戶意見。

功能上的重大更新

除了視覺，功能上的提升主要體現在大螢幕裝置。iPadOS 26 迎來了重大突破，加入了三角形箭頭、自由浮動視窗、視窗控制按鈕和統一的選單欄，這與過去抽象的限制形成鮮明對比。macOS 也獲得了不錯的更新，例如跨裝置共享的即時動態和增強的 Spotlight 搜尋。相較之下，iPhone 的更新則較為次要，主要是一些 Photos App 和充電顯示的小改進。

Vision Pro 與未來策略

Vision Pro 作為生態系統中最大的「螢幕」，其設計令人驚豔，並能與智慧家庭裝置整合。作者推測蘋果未來的策略可能會更側重於大螢幕和沉浸式體驗，這似乎也暗示著摺疊螢幕的可能發展方向。

iPhone 在生態系統中的新角色

更新所有裝置後，作者發現 iPhone 的角色變得有些矛盾，既更重要又似乎沒那麼核心了。Mac 的 Launchpad 中出現了 iPhone 應用程式用於螢幕鏡像，Mac 和 iPad 都有「電話」應用程式來透過 iPhone 撥打電話，甚至 Apple Watch 的一些操作也依賴 iPhone。這使得 iPhone 更像是一個具備通訊和網路能力的終端與入口，而非唯一的互動裝置，帶有物聯網（Internet of Everything）的感覺。

開發者測試版與未來優化

作者強調目前版本是開發者測試版。蘋果可能會透過 HDR 螢幕等方式解決可讀性問題，並進一步優化 iPhone 上的模糊效果和顏色呈現。即便最終 iPhone 的視覺效果不盡如人意，至少功能應能流暢運行。

WWDC 的重要性與結論

作者總結，本次 WWDC 無疑是近年來最重要的一次，展現了蘋果強大的軟硬體整合能力。液態玻璃效果的即時渲染在技術上非常困難，不是簡單換個主題就能實現的。作者認為這可能是蘋果習慣性拋出難題，讓其他廠商難以追趕的策略。因此，作者建議國內其他廠商不要盲目跟隨這個特定的設計趨勢，應堅持自己的目標。

================================================

Everyone who upgrades to iOS 26. is gonna get charged 6 Mi Coins. It's been a while since we've had a WWDC this hyped up, right? It's even. trending on the charts. Usually, Apple's updates. are like WeChat updates. they just tweak a few things here and there every year. Even if you don't like 'em,. you just kinda deal with it. But this year's WWDC? They went all in,. forcing us to embrace this 'liquid glass' look. You gotta use it! Don't like it? Well, get used to it. Do you guys think this style looks good? It's like taking me back to the most carefree times, honestly. The consensus among my studio mates is that. individually, certain parts or details look nice,. but when you put them all together,. it's a chaotic mess,. and the readability is terrible. But after updating a few devices, I was like,. 'Whoa,. something's off here.' I think for a lot of people, their first interaction with the new system is through the. iPhone. But this year's different. I feel like. the iOS 26 series is designed with larger screen devices in mind. The iPhone's kinda the strategic sacrifice here.

Look at the Control Center. on the iPhone, it always feels like the blur effect isn't strong enough. oh. the full effect is a gradual blur. On the iPhone, the icons seem too busy,. but on the iPad, the icons are naturally bigger,. and the exquisite lighting,. layering,. and glass effects. in the design really shine through. On the iPhone,. Man,. Cook should really charge 6 Mi Coins for that. how strong the glass texture is. Even in the lock screen customization,. the light reflects onto the buttons below. It's only on the iPad that. that Apple used to have. But during the keynote,. they said the inspiration for the liquid glass look came from VisionOS. Well, as someone who owns a Vision Pro,. I can tell you. I've even upgraded my Vision Pro to the new system. Just take a look at the materials and textures. How similar do they really look to iOS? I'd say. this whole narrative is just a way. to drive traffic to the Vision Pro, which hasn't been selling all that great,. which has been losing steam lately. the inspiration must've come from HarmonyOS! Look at.

this magnifying glass. does it look familiar? And this global lighting effect. does that ring a bell? The macOS menu finally has that immersive feel too. You know how Apple works. if they think something's really good,. they'll reserve it for the latest models. If it's just okay,. then they'll "decentralize" it. this time, they're being super generous. iPhones from the 11 series and above can all upgrade. I dug out my iPhone 12 and gave it a try. Look at. all these refraction effects,. the little bubbles in the tabs. everything works surprisingly well. There are occasional stutters,. but that's probably just because it's Beta 1. Last year, iOS 18 randomly introduced icon customization,. and iOS 26 finally answers the puzzle. Man,. it's all for these flashy glass effects. Objectively speaking,. they look stunning on their own,. and these folders look even more amazing. it's like an app amber. The most Apple-like thing is,. if you follow their guidelines for icons,. they'll automatically convert them to the new version for you. Which one do you think looks better, the old or the new?

Oh, of course,. they won't ask for your opinion. they'll just force the conversion on you. Even if you ignore the aesthetics,. functionally, it's a feast for large-screen devices. The iPhone's just getting the leftovers, more or less. After years of bashing. and then years of making excuses,. just when everyone's gradually come to terms with Apple's decisions. like designing the mouse cursor as a tiny dot. as if there was some deep thought behind it. iPadOS 26 drops a bombshell. with triangular arrows!!! Free-floating windows!!! Window control buttons!!! A unified menu bar!!! So, were they just messing with us all these past years? Anyway,. when the official version comes out,. just go ahead and upgrade your iPad without hesitation. macOS is also getting some great updates. Now, live activities can be shared across devices,. The iPhone didn't really have any groundbreaking updates,. but they're pulling out some features and making them more useful. Spotlight Search has also been significantly enhanced. You can quickly send emails,. create reminders,. and more. Vision Pro, being the biggest "screen" in Apple's ecosystem,. not only looks stunning by design. permanently.

Pair it with some Mi Home devices,. and it's like transforming a rough space into a polished one. If your neck can handle it,. those a few dozen minutes of augmented reality. are absolutely mind-blowing. With large-screen devices being so popular,. is Apple's future strategy going to. focus more on large-screen and immersive device experiences? It seems like foldable screens might not be far off, huh? In comparison,. on the small-screen device front,. the iPhone has also seen some minor improvements. The Photos app no longer freezes halfway through loading. it's now split into two columns,. and the search bar has been moved to the bottom for easier access. Although they haven't optimized fast charging,. at least now the slow charging is more predictable. And while there's still no swipe-to-go-back gesture,. you can now swipe back. After upgrading all my devices,. I've noticed something paradoxical about the iPhone. it's become both more. and less crucial. The Mac's Launchpad suddenly feels foreign,. but upon closer inspection,. I see iPhone apps mixed in there,. and clicking one initiates screen mirroring. Not to mention,. both Mac and iPad now have a "Phone" app that,. of course, routes calls through the iPhone. Even on.

my Apple Watch,. I spotted a little rocket icon. I doubt they're making the watch a standalone internet device,. and my iPhone springs into action. It's like,. you need an iPhone,. but you don't necessarily have to use it as your main device; it's just a terminal and gateway. with communication and internet capabilities. It's starting to feel a bit like the Internet of Everything, isn't it? But let's be fair. Despite all the complaints flying around,. this is a developer beta. We can already see Apple's approach. For the readability problem,. Apple probably won't just add shadows bluntly; they'll likely use HDR displays to create a visual distinction. The blur effects on the iPhone. and the colors that emerge after blurring will probably be further optimized. at least it should function without a hitch. satisfied with this outcome. This WWDC is undoubtedly. the most significant one in recent years. It showcases Apple's overwhelming ability to integrate hardware and software seamlessly. Putting aesthetics aside,. the real-time rendered liquid glass effect. isn't something you can achieve just by changing a theme. If I'm being a bit conspiracy-minded,. Apple has a habit of throwing out something. particularly challenging out of the blue,. leaving everyone scrambling to catch up.

This liquid glass effect. is a classic example-it's incredibly difficult to pull off,. If you ask me,. don't follow suit! Absolutely don't! It's a trap! They should just stick to their goals. OK. So that's all for this video. If you find this video interesting. Remember to hit that one-click Like, Subscribe & Bell Notification for me! This is very important to me. Then I'm Cherry. See you next time. Bye ~. Even though the iPhone interface has taken a 720-degree turn,. But on the iPad,. that lock screen time? But on the iPad, you can really notice. you can really feel that effortless power. it's nothing alike. and to generate a few more trending topics for WWDC,. If you ask me,. But. or limiting the floating window features in such an abstract way,. and phone calls can be interconnected too. Now, it can almost act like a text-based Siri. but now also lets you pin widgets to the wall. on first-party pages. but one tap,. to solving many of these issues.

By then, even if the iPhone doesn't look stunning,. Though I'm definitely not. and if not done right, it could be a disaster. Domestic manufacturers have already established their own styles. and keep moving forward.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SoFQHTQEG-w

歡迎訂閱切狸~ 在這裡你會看到很多有趣的數碼資訊哦！ | Please don’t forget to subscribe 💋：https://bit.ly/3jQ3429

-------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------

好久沒有討論度這麼高的WWDC了，這不，OS26的熱搜都掛上了。以往果子的更新，就有點像微信更新一樣，每年就動一點點東西，即使你不喜歡，好歹也就這麼用下去了，但是今年 WWDC 直接釜底抽薪，全部給我吃液態玻璃，必須用！不習慣？那你習慣習慣。

往期影片：

https://youtu.be/hC85_d3S1Rg

https://youtu.be/SWYCfjATo3s

https://youtu.be/O5pabQt5njE

#數碼 #apple #iOS26
item.description:
值得閱讀的理由

  • 了解 Apple 全新的「液態玻璃」設計語言及其在不同裝置上的表現差異（尤其是在 iPhone 與 iPad 之間）。
  • 認識 iPadOS 在多視窗功能上的重大突破，以及其他系統（macOS, Spotlight, Photos App）的功能增強。
  • 探討 iPhone 在 Apple 生態系統中角色的可能轉變，從核心裝置轉向更像是終端與入口。
  • 獲得對本次 WWDC 更新的整體評價，包括其技術挑戰、潛在策略以及對未來發展方向的洞察（例如摺疊螢幕的可能性）。

摘要

這一次的 WWDC 引起了很高的討論度，作者提到這是近年來備受期待的一次。更新強行導入了全新的「液態玻璃」設計風格，作者及其工作室的同事認為雖然某些單獨的細節不錯，但整體組合起來顯得混亂，並且影響了可讀性。

設計在不同裝置上的呈現

作者覺得 iOS 26 系列的設計似乎是為大螢幕裝置而生，iPhone 在其中更像是戰略性犧牲品。作者觀察到，同樣的模糊效果和圖標呈現，在 iPad 上能更好地展現設計中的細緻光影、層次感和液態玻璃效果，而 iPhone 上的效果則顯得不夠充分且圖標過於擁擠。

設計靈感的猜測

關於設計靈感，官方宣稱來自 VisionOS。然而，身為 Vision Pro 使用者的作者對此提出質疑，認為實際材質呈現並不相似。作者推測這可能是為了帶動 Vision Pro 的流量，並大膽猜測其靈感實際上可能源自 HarmonyOS，並以類似的放大鏡和全局光效作為例證。

設備相容性與初步表現

令人意外的是，本次更新的設備相容性很高，從 iPhone 11 系列及以上都可以升級。作者用 iPhone 12 進行測試，發現這些折射效果、標籤頁中的小氣泡等細節呈現得相當不錯，儘管作為 Beta 1 版本偶爾會出現卡頓。

設計細節與圖標

作者認為 iOS 26 的設計解答了去年 iOS 18 隨機出現的圖標自定義功能之謎，一切都是為了搭配這次的玻璃效果。這些效果單獨看確實很漂亮，尤其文件夾看起來像是「應用琥珀」。蘋果的典型做法是，只要開發者遵循指南，系統就會自動轉換圖標風格，並且不會徵詢用戶意見。

功能上的重大更新

除了視覺，功能上的提升主要體現在大螢幕裝置。iPadOS 26 迎來了重大突破，加入了三角形箭頭、自由浮動視窗、視窗控制按鈕和統一的選單欄，這與過去抽象的限制形成鮮明對比。macOS 也獲得了不錯的更新，例如跨裝置共享的即時動態和增強的 Spotlight 搜尋。相較之下，iPhone 的更新則較為次要，主要是一些 Photos App 和充電顯示的小改進。

Vision Pro 與未來策略

Vision Pro 作為生態系統中最大的「螢幕」，其設計令人驚豔，並能與智慧家庭裝置整合。作者推測蘋果未來的策略可能會更側重於大螢幕和沉浸式體驗，這似乎也暗示著摺疊螢幕的可能發展方向。

iPhone 在生態系統中的新角色

更新所有裝置後，作者發現 iPhone 的角色變得有些矛盾，既更重要又似乎沒那麼核心了。Mac 的 Launchpad 中出現了 iPhone 應用程式用於螢幕鏡像，Mac 和 iPad 都有「電話」應用程式來透過 iPhone 撥打電話，甚至 Apple Watch 的一些操作也依賴 iPhone。這使得 iPhone 更像是一個具備通訊和網路能力的終端與入口，而非唯一的互動裝置，帶有物聯網（Internet of Everything）的感覺。

開發者測試版與未來優化

作者強調目前版本是開發者測試版。蘋果可能會透過 HDR 螢幕等方式解決可讀性問題，並進一步優化 iPhone 上的模糊效果和顏色呈現。即便最終 iPhone 的視覺效果不盡如人意，至少功能應能流暢運行。

WWDC 的重要性與結論

作者總結，本次 WWDC 無疑是近年來最重要的一次，展現了蘋果強大的軟硬體整合能力。液態玻璃效果的即時渲染在技術上非常困難，不是簡單換個主題就能實現的。作者認為這可能是蘋果習慣性拋出難題，讓其他廠商難以追趕的策略。因此，作者建議國內其他廠商不要盲目跟隨這個特定的設計趨勢，應堅持自己的目標。

================================================

Everyone who upgrades to iOS 26. is gonna get charged 6 Mi Coins. It's been a while since we've had a WWDC this hyped up, right? It's even. trending on the charts. Usually, Apple's updates. are like WeChat updates. they just tweak a few things here and there every year. Even if you don't like 'em,. you just kinda deal with it. But this year's WWDC? They went all in,. forcing us to embrace this 'liquid glass' look. You gotta use it! Don't like it? Well, get used to it. Do you guys think this style looks good? It's like taking me back to the most carefree times, honestly. The consensus among my studio mates is that. individually, certain parts or details look nice,. but when you put them all together,. it's a chaotic mess,. and the readability is terrible. But after updating a few devices, I was like,. 'Whoa,. something's off here.' I think for a lot of people, their first interaction with the new system is through the. iPhone. But this year's different. I feel like. the iOS 26 series is designed with larger screen devices in mind. The iPhone's kinda the strategic sacrifice here.

Look at the Control Center. on the iPhone, it always feels like the blur effect isn't strong enough. oh. the full effect is a gradual blur. On the iPhone, the icons seem too busy,. but on the iPad, the icons are naturally bigger,. and the exquisite lighting,. layering,. and glass effects. in the design really shine through. On the iPhone,. Man,. Cook should really charge 6 Mi Coins for that. how strong the glass texture is. Even in the lock screen customization,. the light reflects onto the buttons below. It's only on the iPad that. that Apple used to have. But during the keynote,. they said the inspiration for the liquid glass look came from VisionOS. Well, as someone who owns a Vision Pro,. I can tell you. I've even upgraded my Vision Pro to the new system. Just take a look at the materials and textures. How similar do they really look to iOS? I'd say. this whole narrative is just a way. to drive traffic to the Vision Pro, which hasn't been selling all that great,. which has been losing steam lately. the inspiration must've come from HarmonyOS! this magnifying glass. does it look familiar? And this global lighting effect. does that ring a bell? The macOS menu finally has that immersive feel too. You know how Apple works. if they think something's really good,. they'll reserve it for the latest models. If it's just okay,. then they'll "decentralize" it. this time, they're being super generous. iPhones from the 11 series and above can all upgrade. I dug out my iPhone 12 and gave it a try. Look at. all these refraction effects,. the little bubbles in the tabs. everything works surprisingly well. There are occasional stutters,. but that's probably just because it's Beta 1. Last year, iOS 18 randomly introduced icon customization,. and iOS 26 finally answers the puzzle. Man,. it's all for these flashy glass effects. Objectively speaking,. they look stunning on their own,. and these folders look even more amazing. it's like an app amber. The most Apple-like thing is,. if you follow their guidelines for icons,. they'll automatically convert them to the new version for you. Which one do you think looks better, the old or the new?

Oh, of course,. they won't ask for your opinion. they'll just force the conversion on you. Even if you ignore the aesthetics,. functionally, it's a feast for large-screen devices. The iPhone's just getting the leftovers, more or less. After years of bashing. and then years of making excuses,. just when everyone's gradually come to terms with Apple's decisions. like designing the mouse cursor as a tiny dot. as if there was some deep thought behind it. iPadOS 26 drops a bombshell. with triangular arrows!!! Free-floating windows!!! Window control buttons!!! A unified menu bar!!! So, were they just messing with us all these past years? Anyway,. when the official version comes out,. just go ahead and upgrade your iPad without hesitation. macOS is also getting some great updates. Now, live activities can be shared across devices,. The iPhone didn't really have any groundbreaking updates,. but they're pulling out some features and making them more useful. Spotlight Search has also been significantly enhanced. You can quickly send emails,. create reminders,. and more. Vision Pro, being the biggest "screen" in Apple's ecosystem,. not only looks stunning by design. permanently.

Pair it with some Mi Home devices,. and it's like transforming a rough space into a polished one. If your neck can handle it,. those a few dozen minutes of augmented reality. are absolutely mind-blowing. With large-screen devices being so popular,. is Apple's future strategy going to. focus more on large-screen and immersive device experiences? It seems like foldable screens might not be far off, huh? In comparison,. on the small-screen device front,. the iPhone has also seen some minor improvements. The Photos app no longer freezes halfway through loading. it's now split into two columns,. and the search bar has been moved to the bottom for easier access. Although they haven't optimized fast charging,. at least now the slow charging is more predictable. And while there's still no swipe-to-go-back gesture,. you can now swipe back. After upgrading all my devices,. I've noticed something paradoxical about the iPhone. it's become both more. and less crucial. The Mac's Launchpad suddenly feels foreign,. but upon closer inspection,. I see iPhone apps mixed in there,. and clicking one initiates screen mirroring. Not to mention,. both Mac and iPad now have a "Phone" app that,. of course, routes calls through the iPhone. Even on.

my Apple Watch,. I spotted a little rocket icon. I doubt they're making the watch a standalone internet device,. and my iPhone springs into action. It's like,. you need an iPhone,. but you don't necessarily have to use it as your main device; it's just a terminal and gateway. with communication and internet capabilities. It's starting to feel a bit like the Internet of Everything, isn't it? But let's be fair. Despite all the complaints flying around,. this is a developer beta. We can already see Apple's approach. For the readability problem,. Apple probably won't just add shadows bluntly; they'll likely use HDR displays to create a visual distinction. The blur effects on the iPhone. and the colors that emerge after blurring will probably be further optimized. at least it should function without a hitch. satisfied with this outcome. Putting aesthetics aside,. the real-time rendered liquid glass effect. isn't something you can achieve just by changing a theme. If I'm being a bit conspiracy-minded,. Apple has a habit of throwing out something. particularly challenging out of the blue,. leaving everyone scrambling to catch up.

This liquid glass effect. is a classic example-it's incredibly difficult to pull off,. If you ask me,. don't follow suit! Absolutely don't! It's a trap! They should just stick to their goals. OK. But on the iPad, you can really notice. you can really feel that effortless power. it's nothing alike. and to generate a few more trending topics for WWDC,. If you ask me,. But. or limiting the floating window features in such an abstract way,. and phone calls can be interconnected too. Now, it can almost act like a text-based Siri. but now also lets you pin widgets to the wall. on first-party pages. but one tap,. to solving many of these issues.

關於設計靈感，官方宣稱來自 VisionOS。然而，身為 Vision Pro 使用者的作者對此提出質疑，認為實際材質呈現並不相似。作者推測這可能是為了帶動 Vision Pro 的流量，並大膽猜測其靈感實際上可能源自 HarmonyOS，並以類似的放大鏡和全局光效作為例證。

設備相容性與初步表現

令人意外的是，本次更新的設備相容性很高，從 iPhone 11 系列及以上都可以升級。作者用 iPhone 12 進行測試，發現這些折射效果、標籤頁中的小氣泡等細節呈現得相當不錯，儘管作為 Beta 1 版本偶爾會出現卡頓。

設計細節與圖標

作者認為 iOS 26 的設計解答了去年 iOS 18 隨機出現的圖標自定義功能之謎，一切都是為了搭配這次的玻璃效果。這些效果單獨看確實很漂亮，尤其文件夾看起來像是「應用琥珀」。蘋果的典型做法是，只要開發者遵循指南，系統就會自動轉換圖標風格，並且不會徵詢用戶意見。

功能上的重大更新

除了視覺，功能上的提升主要體現在大螢幕裝置。iPadOS 26 迎來了重大突破，加入了三角形箭頭、自由浮動視窗、視窗控制按鈕和統一的選單欄，這與過去抽象的限制形成鮮明對比。macOS 也獲得了不錯的更新，例如跨裝置共享的即時動態和增強的 Spotlight 搜尋。相較之下，iPhone 的更新則較為次要，主要是一些 Photos App 和充電顯示的小改進。

Vision Pro 與未來策略

Vision Pro 作為生態系統中最大的「螢幕」，其設計令人驚豔，並能與智慧家庭裝置整合。作者推測蘋果未來的策略可能會更側重於大螢幕和沉浸式體驗，這似乎也暗示著摺疊螢幕的可能發展方向。

iPhone 在生態系統中的新角色

更新所有裝置後，作者發現 iPhone 的角色變得有些矛盾，既更重要又似乎沒那麼核心了。Mac 的 Launchpad 中出現了 iPhone 應用程式用於螢幕鏡像，Mac 和 iPad 都有「電話」應用程式來透過 iPhone 撥打電話，甚至 Apple Watch 的一些操作也依賴 iPhone。這使得 iPhone 更像是一個具備通訊和網路能力的終端與入口，而非唯一的互動裝置，帶有物聯網（Internet of Everything）的感覺。

開發者測試版與未來優化

作者強調目前版本是開發者測試版。蘋果可能會透過 HDR 螢幕等方式解決可讀性問題，並進一步優化 iPhone 上的模糊效果和顏色呈現。即便最終 iPhone 的視覺效果不盡如人意，至少功能應能流暢運行。

WWDC 的重要性與結論

作者總結，本次 WWDC 無疑是近年來最重要的一次，展現了蘋果強大的軟硬體整合能力。液態玻璃效果的即時渲染在技術上非常困難，不是簡單換個主題就能實現的。作者認為這可能是蘋果習慣性拋出難題，讓其他廠商難以追趕的策略。因此，作者建議國內其他廠商不要盲目跟隨這個特定的設計趨勢，應堅持自己的目標。
